_comment: Copy this file to config.json (the setup wizard does it for you). DO NOT commit config.json - it contains your GitHub token.
_signing_comment: Optional anti-spoofing. Run `python generate_keys.py`, put the PRIVATE key here and the PUBLIC key in the app. Leave empty to publish unsigned (current behaviour).
_interval_comment: How often a full collect/test/publish cycle starts. 60 = once an hour, as designed. Cycles never overlap: if one runs long, the next starts right after it finishes.
_dashboard_comment: Local web console (logs, server table, delete, update). install.sh fills in a random dashboard_token. Open http://SERVER_IP:8787/?token=THE_TOKEN. Empty token = no auth (only behind a firewall/SSH tunnel).

test._publish_comment: Only servers whose real measured delay is <= publish_max_ping (ms) are published to the gist. Keeps the app's list genuinely fast. max_ping is the looser test-time cutoff (kept a little above publish_max_ping so a borderline server's warm sample can still qualify); publish_max_ping is the strict publish cutoff that actually decides what ships.
test._accuracy_comment: measure_concurrency caps how many delay probes run at once across ALL batches. This is what keeps pings ACCURATE: too high saturates the server and every ping is wrong. 24-48 is a good range on a normal VPS; lower = more accurate but slower. ping_samples re-probes each survivor and keeps the fastest (min) so a one-off spike doesn't misrank a good server.
test._geo_comment: geo_via_tunnel asks Cloudflare's trace endpoint THROUGH each tunnel to learn the real exit IP + country, so CDN-fronted/chained configs get the correct flag (not the misleading address-based country). Turn off only if it slows the cycle too much.
